title: mentha × nemorosa subsp. timbalii
intro: Mentha × nemorosa subsp. timbalii, also known as bergamot mint, is a hybrid subspecies of mint that is a cross between Mentha aquatica and Mentha longifolia. It is characterized by its distinctive aroma and flavor, often used in herbal remedies and culinary applications. Bergamot mint is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to ease digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial effects. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, to promote overall health and well-being, including benefits like improved digestion, reduced stress, and enhanced mood.
